trade.summary: The Stati Uniti is Svizzera's largest single-country trading partner. Svizzera exports a large surplus driven by pharmaceuticals, chemicals, watches, and financial services. The Stati Uniti exports aircraft, machinery, and IT products. Bilateral trade is underpinned by shared economic values and deep investment ties.
disputes: Stati Uniti pressure on Swiss banking secrecy (largely resolved via FATCA agreement)

diplomatic.summary: Strong bilateral relations built on shared democratic values and deep economic ties. Svizzera represents Stati Uniti interests in countries where Washington lacks diplomatic missions (Cuba, Iran historically). The Stati Uniti is Svizzera's most important partner outside Europe. Swiss neutrality occasionally creates divergence on sanctions policy.

military.summary: Svizzera is neutral and not a NATO member, but shares security values with the Stati Uniti. Stati Uniti military has historically used Swiss airspace. Swiss defense purchases include Stati Uniti equipment. Intelligence sharing occurs through informal channels. No joint exercises.

financial.summary: Massive bilateral financial ties. Stati Uniti is the largest source of FDI into Svizzera and vice versa. Swiss banks have major Stati Uniti operations; Stati Uniti banks and investment firms operate in Zurich and Geneva. FATCA framework governs tax information exchange. Bilateral investment stock exceeds Stati UnitiD 300 billion.

energy.summary: Limited direct energy trade. Svizzera imports some Stati Uniti LNG through European networks. Stati Uniti energy companies (ExxonMobil, Chevron) have Swiss offices. Cooperation on clean energy innovation.

scientific.summary: Among the strongest bilateral scientific partnerships globally. ETH Zurich and Stati Uniti research universities (MIT, Stanford, Caltech) maintain deep programs. Major pharmaceutical R&D collaboration. Joint Nobel laureates. Svizzera is among the top recipients of Stati Uniti-Swiss joint research grants.
